stem: A clinical study study measures the diastolic blood pressure (mmHg) for a sample of 25 patients. The sample mean is 44.2 mmHg with a sample standard deviation of 16.2 mmHg. However, 2 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 23 observations have a mean of 42.1 mmHg and standard deviation of 11.7 mmHg. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 44.2, 95% CI = [37.5, 50.9]. Without outliers: mean = 42.1, 95% CI = [37.2, 47.0]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [37.2, 50.9]. Point estimate: 43.2 mmHg.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [37.2, 50.9].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
